question.text: Seema transfers her mutual fund portfolio worth INR 40 lakh to an irrevocable trust she created for her daughter. Under Section 47(iii) of the Income Tax Act, what is the capital gains tax implication of this transfer?

answer.text: Capital gains tax is payable based on the market value at the date of transfer
Transfer under an irrevocable trust is exempt from capital gains under Sec 47(iii)
Capital gains tax is deferred until the beneficiary sells the assets
Capital gains tax is payable at a flat rate of 10% for trust transfers

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Sec 47(iii), transfer of assets to an irrevocable trust is NOT treated as a transfer for capital gains purposes — hence it is exempt. However, transfer to a revocable trust attracts capital gains tax. Testamentary trusts (created via will) are also exempt. **The Trap:** Candidates may confuse revocable and irrevocable trust tax treatment. The key distinction: irrevocable = exempt from capital gains; revocable = capital gains apply AND income taxed to settlor. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM X-B tests capital gains implications of trust transfers. This is also relevant to NISM Series V-A where capital gains taxation of mutual funds is tested. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** An irrevocable trust is a powerful estate planning tool precisely because of Sec 47(iii) exemption. But remind clients: once assets are in an irrevocable trust (Sec 77-78), they CANNOT be taken back.
